Key Takeaways:
o “Launch List” defined: a list that you build for (and use during) a launch
o For a simple example, let’s assume you already have a list:
o They’re opted in to this list
o Now you’re going into a launch, so you create a launch list
o You create this other list and set it up through your list host
o To start your launch, you send your main list into a squeeze page - they’ll
see a video training for your launch (PLC)
o When they opt in they get to see your PLC’s and eventually your sales
video/letter
o So, ultimately, why are we sending people to another squeeze page and asking
them to opt in again? Because when you go into a launch the offer is different from
what they initially signed up for - you’re also going to be emailing them a lot more
o If you add up all the email, you’ll be sending during the launch it’s between 10-15
emails. By putting them through the process of joining your launch list they’ll be
interested and not dislike getting your emails
o Also, if your Prelaunch Content is great they’ll love you by the end. Don’t be afraid
of sending people a lot of email, especially if they’ve asked for it by opting in to your
launch list
o The people that do opt in are so much warmer - it rejuvenates them and creates
recency. Is it possible to skip this and just send PLC to your main list? Yes, you
absolutely can
o If the offer is very relevant to what they initially opted in for you could just send
your whole list the entire launch. However, in general it’s preferred to put them
into a launch list because they’re much more powerful afterward

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Let's talk about your launch list.
First of all, let's define it.
Your launch list is a list that you build for and during a launch.
Let me first diagram it out for you. Let's just say you already have a list. Here is your
list. People have lists for all different types of things - they have a newsletter list, EZ list, they
have promotion list or whatever - but this is your list.
You have to put people on your list and you would be sending them some messages,
whether they’re auto-responder messages or whether they’d be newsletters or whatever
you’ve been sending them some email or maybe they opted in and you haven’t been sending
them anything whatsoever and they have been sitting there accumulating. That’s fine. I
mean it’s not fine, it’s not what you should be doing but it doesn’t matter for this discussion
because now you’re about to go in to a launch and you’re going to create a launch list.
Let’s say your list is about playing guitars, learning to play blues guitar. Now you are
going to do a launch for a product about pentatonic guitar skills.
What you would typically do is create this other list over here. With almost any list
host you can create as many different lists and sub lists that you want.
What you do is you’re mailing, mailing, mailing. Then let's say someone is here when
you’re ready to start your launch you would mail these people into a squeeze page.
You send them to the squeeze page and you say, “Hey. I’ve got this really cool video
for you about playing the pentatonic scale. Plus, I’ve got some downloads for you and some
great training and it’s 100%. Just go here.”
You send the squeeze page and after they opt in then they go into your launch list.
Then you would send them PLC1, the whole sideway-sales, PLC2, PLC3-we call is
one, two, three. This is your Prelaunch Content and of course eventually they’re going to get
your sales video or sales letter.
Why would you do this? Why would you bother having people that have already
subscribed to you, are already on your list? Why would you send them to a squeeze page and
ask them to opt in yet again?
The reason is because they joined for some type of promise originally. When they
came to your site, there was some promise that said, “Opt in and we’ll send you this.”
Generally, when you into a launch, the offer there is slightly different and you’re
going to be sending them emails at different frequency. Maybe they’re getting email once a
week over here or every other week or two times a week but once you get them into a launch,
you’re probably going to be emailing them a lot more. You’ll be emailing for each piece of
Prelaunch Content. You’re probably mailing them in between each piece of Prelaunch
Content.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Once you Open Cart here and you have your cart opened for five to seven days, you’re
going to be emailing them probably two times on that first day and then you’re going to be
emailing them each day, then on the closing day you’re probably going to be emailing them
two or even three times. If you add up all the email you’re going to send them during the
launch, you’re probably going to send them in the order of at least 10, 12, maybe 15 emails.
Now, a lot of people are like, “Well, wouldn’t that make them mad?
Wouldn’t they unsubscribe from you? The answer is no, they won’t if they’re
interested in getting the material. The way that you know that they’re
interested is because they went through this process of joining your new
launch list.
Now a lot of times people think, “Yeah, they think I’m just going to make them mad.
They’re going to hate me because I’m sending so much email.” The reality is like I showed
you in that last video, they actually love you.
When you take them through this process and do this sideways sales letter and
deliver some real value in the Prelaunch Content especially if they are great videos and great
training, maybe you have some downloads or something. They come out in the end whether
they buy from your or not, they love you. That’s why we see that great launch list
phenomenon.
That’s why in that last email, you have might wondered, “Why is he talking about all
these JVs and who cares about this big guru launches or whatever?” I was just showing you
graphically how I’ve seen this over and over that the warmest, the best list that you can have
is that launch list. We see is over and over in these JV competitions.
Don’t be afraid of sending people a lot of email especially if they’ve asked for that
email by joining your launch list. Then typically even if they do unsubscribe, they’ll
unsubscribe from the launch list and they’ll still be on your main list. Basically the
launch list is a way of getting people’s permission to send them this promotion
essentially. It’s getting them to raise their hand.
Now let’s just say you have 10,000 people and there’s a last story here. I know a lot of
people are watching this and be like, “10,000 people. Why would I even bother for such a
tiny list. I got like 200,000.” There’s a lot of other people that are saying, “Oh, man. I don’t
have a list. I’m never going to have 10,000.” It doesn’t matter. I’m just using that as an
example. It’s going to make the math easier for me.
Typically, if you go through something like this it completely depends, of course on
that recent RFM model, how warm this list is, what your relationship is, how recently they’ve
joined your list. A lot of times out of those 10,000 you might only get 2,000 to move over to
your launch list. I’ve seen it where it was like 80% of the people went over. Typically, if 10 or
20% of your list join in your launch list, you’re doing pretty good. These people are going to
be pure gold because they are very much interested in this.
The test I’ve shown is just getting people to go through this process, these people are
more responsive just having gone through this squeeze process than they would have been if
those same 2,000 people were over on this list. It rejuvenates them. It’s that recency again.
By going through this process of raising their hand and saying yes, they’ve gone through that
recency process and now they’re that much warmer. Frankly I see some serious crazy
numbers that these people are worth once they go through that process.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Is it possible to do a launch and not bother with this re-opt in process and just to
start sending out your Prelaunch Content over here to your main list? Absolutely you can.
If this launch is going to be for a product that is very much in alignment to what they
opted in for. In other words, if they opted in for how to grow tomatoes, and then your launch
is going to be how to grow heirloom tomatoes, very very similar match, you could just go
ahead and send your entire list the entire launch, you could.
If whatever the launch is going to be for is directly in alignment with what they
originally joined your list for, you could do that especially if you have never done any kind of
a launch before, maybe you send your entire list through the entire sequence, but in general
I prefer to run people through a squeeze page and start a new launch list.
Like I said that launch list is like this warm fuzzy blanket because they are so
powerful once people go through that process and join your launch list.
